verifyCommand: npm run check-types && npm run test && npm run benchmark
acceptanceCriteria: npm run test passes (vitest, all files)
npm run benchmark passes 14/14 with no regression
All non-skipped features have passes: true

features.title: Baseline perf instrumentation for lib-file double-load
acceptance_criteria: benchmark/run-benchmark.ts records per-fixture timing including lib-load cost
Baseline numbers (cold lib-load in validatorInProcess + tsLanguageServiceFixer) written to plans/progress.md
npm run benchmark still 14/14; check-types clean
features.notes: ARCHITECTURE.md section 9: lib files parsed twice (~600-800ms + ~200ms). Quantify before refactoring.

features.title: Shared Program/host abstraction so Layer 0 and Layer 1 share one lib-file parse
acceptance_criteria: validatorInProcess.ts (Layer 0) and tsLanguageServiceFixer.ts (Layer 1) consume a single shared lib-file parse / host
Diagnostics produced are byte-identical to pre-refactor (regression-tested)
Measured per-fixture latency improves vs T-3c-1 baseline (record in progress.md)
npm run test passes; npm run benchmark 14/14; check-types clean
features.notes: ARCHITECTURE.md section 12 D2. Layer 1 uses LanguageServiceHost, Layer 0 uses CompilerHost - unify the host abstraction. Do NOT bundle typescript; keep the workspace lib-path bet intact (see plans/guardrails.md SIGN-102).

features.title: Document shared-Program decision in ARCHITECTURE.md and ROADMAP.md
acceptance_criteria: ARCHITECTURE.md section 9 (perf) and section 12 D2 updated to reflect the shipped shared-Program approach
ROADMAP.md Phase 3c marked done with the measured latency delta

features.title: fixtures/REAL.md documenting real-failure fixture format + node_modules strategy
acceptance_criteria: fixtures/REAL.md exists: real-<timestamp>-<hash>/ layout, expected.json schema, mustPass lifecycle (false then true once fixed)
Documents node_modules strategy option (a): commit broken .ts(x) + package-lock.json + setup.sh (per ROADMAP 3b)
features.notes: ROADMAP Phase 3b. so-real-* fixtures already exist; this formalizes the real-failure capture format.

features.title: Enhance scripts/capture-fixture.mjs to emit real-failure fixture dirs
acceptance_criteria: capture-fixture.mjs writes fixtures/real-<timestamp>-<hash>/ with broken files, Diagnostic[] snapshot, and expected.json (mustPass:false default) + setup.sh
A smoke/unit test exercises the capture flow on a sample broken workspace
Existing benchmark fixture discovery is unaffected; check-types/test/benchmark green
features.notes: Reuse existing scripts/capture-fixture.mjs. expected.json schema already in repo (fixtures/*/expected.json).

features.title: Benchmark harness treats mustPass:false fixtures as report-only (non-blocking)
acceptance_criteria: benchmark/run-benchmark.ts reads expected.json mustPass; mustPass:false fixtures are reported but do not fail the run/CI
Add/convert one seed real-* fixture (mustPass:false) to validate the report-only path end-to-end
npm run benchmark stays 14/14 on mustPass:true set; check-types/test green
features.notes: Lets production-captured failures live in-tree before a fix ships, then flip mustPass:true once fixed.
